title: Social Behavior and Social Play
description: Assessment of early social and play behaviors involving peers.

title: Social 5-M
description: Spontaneously follows peers or imitates their motor behavior 2 times. (e.g., follows a peer into a playhouse). (TO: 30 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously follows peers or imitates their motor behavior 2 times during a 30-minute observation. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously follows peers or imitates their motor behavior 1 time during a 30-minute observation.

title: MOTOR IMITATION — LEVEL 1
description: Assessment of a child's ability to imitate the gross motor behaviors, fine motor behaviors, and actions with objects modeled by others.

title: Motor Imitation 1-M
description: Imitates 2 gross motor movements when prompted with, Do this (e.g., Clapping, raising arms). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates 2 gross motor movements presented by an adult. Even if the responses are approximations, score them as correct. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he imitates only one motor action. Don’t give any points if the child always emits the same behavior, such as clapping.

title: Motor Imitation 2-M
description: Imitates 4 gross motor movements when prompted with, Do this. (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates 4 gross motor movements presented by an adult. Even if the responses are approximations, score them as correct. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he imitates 3 motor actions.

title: Motor Imitation 3-M
description: Imitates 8 motor movements, 2 of which involve objects (e.g., shaking a maraca, tapping sticks together). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates 6 motor movements presented by an adult, and can imitate 2 adult motor behaviors involving objects (a total of 8 imitations). Even if the responses are approximations, score them as correct. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he imitates 6 behaviors of any type.

title: Motor Imitation 4-M
description: Spontaneously imitates the motor behaviors of others on 5 occasions. (O)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously imitates the motor behaviors of others on 5 occasions. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously imitates the motor behaviors of others on 2 occasions.

title: Motor Imitation 5-M
description: Imitates 20 motor movements of any type (e.g., fine motor, gross motor, imitation with objects). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates 20 motor behaviors of any type. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he imitates 15 motor behaviors of any type.

title: EARLY ECHOIC SKILLS ASSESSMENT (EESA)
description: Evaluates a child’s ability to repeat a speech model, including speech phonemes, syllable combinations, and intonation patterns typically acquired between birth and 30 months of age.
